name: MAX30102
fullName: MAX30102 Pulse Oximeter & Heart Rate Sensor Module
tagline: Medical-grade optical sensor for SpO2 and heart rate — I2C, 1.8V core
description: The MAX30102 is a high-sensitivity optical biosensor module integrating a red LED (660nm), IR LED (880nm), photodetector, and analog front-end in one package. Used in pulse oximeters and wearable health monitors. Outputs raw PPG (photoplethysmography) data over I2C which must be processed in firmware to extract BPM and SpO2.

pinout.name: INT
pinout.desc: Interrupt output (active LOW) — optional, for FIFO-driven reads

wiringNote: I2C address is fixed at 0x57 — cannot be changed. If using two MAX30102 sensors (dual-site PPG), they must be on separate I2C buses (use ESP32's Wire and Wire1). Do NOT use them on the same bus.
commonMistakes: Using two sensors on the same I2C bus — address conflict, both fail
Not placing finger flat on sensor — reflective PPG needs firm contact
Reading raw IR and calling it SpO2 — you need both red AND IR + R-ratio calculation for SpO2
Using Wire.begin() without specifying SDA/SCL pins on ESP32
